transcript: Tailor shop, can I help you?

Uh, yeah, I’ve got a video shoot comin’ up. We’re gonna be shooting a video and I need to treat some different materials and fabrics. I’ve been tryin’ to get through and I haven’t been able to reach ya, so I’m glad I got through. My name’s Oscar.
So, what can I do for you?
I need to acid wash some tweed. Can we do something like that, together, with you?
I don’t have ‘em in—with me. I have to order. I don’t have tweed. I have wool; lotta wool., but not tweed—
—Oh! [delay & pitch] Um, could we zipper some spandex? Could we add a zipper to some spandex garments that I’ve got, if I brought those in?
Uh, bring and let me check ‘em, if we can do ‘em.
—ruffle some velour. Can you add a ruffle to it if I brought it to you, for some clothes?
No. The velourian [?], I don’t have ‘em.
No, I’ll bring you the velour. Can you just ruffle it for me on the sleeves?
Uh, y—you mean you wanna put ‘em on the sleeves?
Yeah, put some ruffle in there. Uh-huh.
Oh! [delay & pitch] Excellent! Um, okay! Could you do some, maybe, pleated canvas for me, if I brought that in to ya? [inhales]
You know, the problem—I’m very busy. I don’t have time enough for those things. To be honest with you, I’m very, very busy. I can’t—I can’t spend time, you know, on those things.
I’ll give you a deposit. I can send ya—
—It’s not the money! It’s not the money!
I’m very, very busy; it’s not the money.

Hey, well, guy, we got a video shoot comin’ up. There’s twenty-five people on set! You know, we need to have silhouettes; we need to have a lot of stuff draped over here! You follow me? [delay & pitch]
I know. Yeah, I know! I know.
Okay. Listen, can you, um, cuff some gingham, for me? Add some cuffs to it?
Oh! [delay & pitch] Fantastic! I need to come by about six o’clock tomorrow morning to see ya.
You gonna be there at five-thirty, six A.M.?
No, I’ll be here, you know, 7:30.
Seven-thirty. Any way you can meet me at 6:30 in the morning?
No? [sighs] [delay & pitch] Well, how late are you open?
Now, I wanna make, like, a woven pattern on some polyester. Kinda woven throughout. Just for the silhouette, you understand. Can you add something like that, for me, as a layer, as a lining?
You need somebody to do ‘em for you. I don’t have the time for that. You know I’m—I’m almost—I’m gonna close my shop. You know, I’m in this business 51 years; I’m sick and tired of it, honestly.
Oh yeah! I’m—I’m finished from it. I’m finished from it—
—You’re gonna love what ya see! I got a video. You wanna be in the video? We’re gonna put you in the video—
—I don’t—I don’t need anything. I’m 70 years old; I don’t care about anything anymore. I wanna take a rest.
—I’m gonna send you a credit card payment.
It’s not the money, no, no. I’m—I’m not looking for the money. I—I’m looking for my rest, to take a rest.
You looking for young girls, or…
I am young, but, um, 70 years old is not young anymore.
I’m gonna bring some young girls in there—
—You’re gonna love ‘em! They’ve got towels, they’ve got blankets, they’ve got blouses on; you’re gonna love ‘em!
No. What that gonna do for me?
Doesn’t matter; I have a lotta girls [laughs]
Yeah. I have a lot of customers [unintelliglble]—
—Not like this! These girls have linens, they have stingray leather with buttons; they’re gonna open their buttons. Oh, bigtime! [chuckles] [delay & pitch]
Okay, listen. Just ruffle the velour. I’ll meet you at seven A.M., seven A.M. shārp tomorrow. And I need that ruffled, okay? First thing tomorrow.
Who gonna do it for you in the morning? Are you kidding?
Who gonna work for you on—on this time?
—Nobo—Nobody’s gonna work for you, man, like that. No, no, no, no. Forget it!
—You’re very particular about what time you open and I said, “Okay. I’ll be there at seven o’clock”—
—Yeah, but you need somebody to sew for you, man.
Yes! We got a video shoot comin’ up!—
—Yeah! You need somebody to sew; I don’t have somebody to sew for you.
No. We need you to be there; seven o’clock. I’ll give ya a cash deposit.
—We need you to be there! Sev—
—I told you; it’s not the money.
What do you mean, you don’t want the money? I’ll give you money; cash money. I’ll put you in the video—
—It’s not the money! It’s not the—I’m not looking for the money; I’m looking for my—to be free.
Oh, no! No, no; I don’t need anybody. I have [unintelliglible]—
—Look, we need to add absorption. We—these clothes need absorption; don’t you understand that?
I—I—I understand everything, but I don’t have the time right now—
—These clothes need absorption; don’t you understand that? How much time do you need? [exhales]
Listen, I don’t need your money, okay? I don’t need money.
You wanna be in the video? I’m gonna put you—
No, no! I—I don—I’m not gonna be in the video, no.
It’s gonna be on YouTube! Th—hundreds and hundreds of views!—
—Doesn’t make any difference. Whatever you put, I don’t give a damn!
You’re gonna be with some dancers—
Listen, I—I don’t have time, now, for you. Buh-bye!
Listen, I need this stuff ruffled, big time, here!
Tailor shop. Can I help you?
—Please just help me ruffle this velour, one time, okay? That’s the one thing I need here, today; I could bring it in today—
—Listen, I told you, I don’t have time, you know, for that. I have enough job over here. I don’t have the time. If you could wait—call—for couple weeks I will help you.
I’ll just bring in one jacket; it’s one jacket.—
—I’m very busy; i don’t take anything right now.
I’ll send you a credit card deposit online!
—I’m very, very busy; I don’t have the time for this kind of job.
We just wanna get ruffled, here, guy; that’s all we need [exhales]
Check—find somebody else, man. S—find somebody else.
Can’t you just ruffle our feathers a little bit, here, today, please, this afternoon?
You don’t understand what that mean, “no”?
You guys open for business up ‘ere?
Yeah, I—I have—I been here 50 years. Yes, sir.
Well, fella, I’ve been on the job for 14 years!
Okay, good for you! Find somebody else!
—just take my credit card; lemme give you my credit card number.
W—you got a pen? I’ll give you a minute to get a pen.
Look, guy, we need to get ruffled here! Whadda ya want me to say? [exhales]
